Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01) for clients currently on the stipend platform by looking for records with a non-null Monthly Stipend Fee using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records. Discover that 4 clients have active stipend subscriptions: Canopy Health ($2,000/month, AM: Catalina Dubois), Vectral Systems ($2,000/month, AM: Mina Hashimoto), GreenStack Energy ($2,000/month, AM: Emeka Diallo), and BrightLoop Analytics ($2,000/month, AM: Mina Hashimoto). Also discover Terraform Digital ($2,000/month, AM: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Onboarding).
OE 2: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) for Vectral Systems records using airtable_search_records to discover the actual policy limits that were applied. Key findings: ergonomic equipment limit is $600 (Danielle Osei's $475 chair approved; review notes reference "Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment"), furniture default was stale at $400 (Maria Santos $550 desk rejected despite $600 being the real limit), internet limit is $150 (Kevin Tran's $185 exceeded even the correct limit but was approved as goodwill), and coworking day passes were rejected per client agreement.
OE 3: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for Mosaic Robotics records using airtable_search_records to discover Mosaic's policy configuration. Key findings: furniture/ergonomic cap is $800 (Andre Washington's $1,200 desk auto-approved in error due to string comparison bug max_amount stored as string "800"), coworking is explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy (Andre Washington's $350 membership and Kenji Nakamura's $135 day passes both auto-approved due to excluded_categories stored as string instead of array).
OE 4: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for BrightLoop Analytics records using airtable_search_records to find BrightLoop's stipend submissions. Discover Jordan Ekwueme's approved $299.99 WiFi system and $45 monitor stand (flagged with PDF template match to the TechComfort Supply pattern), plus the Canopy Health and GreenStack stipend patterns for a complete picture of per-client limits.
OE 5: Search Airtable Relocations table (tblRelocations01) for Vectral Systems records using airtable_search_records to find Camille Oduya's relocation record (recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle) with the special requirement: "ENHANCED STIPEND: $3,500 home-office equipment stipend (vs. standard $2,000) negotiated as part of Camille's offer package MUST be configured in stipend platform from day one."
OE 6: Search CRM for Terraform Digital using crm_search_companies to find the company record, discovering it is also tracked internally as "Vantage Distributed" in legacy CRM records. Retrieve account details: 1,200+ employees, $85M annual revenue, Enterprise contract signed March 18, 2026, monthly stipend platform fee of $2,000, and 3 relocations requested (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi). Discover that volume pricing tiers are being formalized (standard rate for 1-10 moves/quarter, 8% discount at 11-20 moves).
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Terraform" or "onboarding" or "stipend platform" to find discussions about the Terraform Digital setup in #customer-engagement (C002) and #engineering (C003). Discover any threads about the stipend platform configuration needed for Terraform's 1,200+ employee self-serve onboarding and volume pricing tier requirements.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Terraform" or "stipend" or "Camille" or "enhanced stipend" to find correspondence about the Terraform Digital onboarding requirements (Rachel Nguyen's relocation requests) and any Vectral emails referencing Camille Oduya's negotiated $3,500 enhanced stipend arrangement.
OE 9: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "expense" or "config" or "policy" or "validation" in #engineering (C003) to find Lena Björkström's discussions about the config pipeline fixes (string-vs-number type mismatch for Vectral, excluded_categories string-vs-array for Mosaic) and Samira's launch readiness checklist discussions.
OE 10: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "expense" or "config" or "policy" to find Lena's existing config fix tickets (Vectral policy config fix and Mosaic config fix), Dmitri's audit ticket (47-expense audit), and ENG-220 (duplicate receipt detection). These are the tickets Anh needs to add comments to.
OE 11: Look up Lena Björkström's contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Lena" to retrieve her email address lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com for sending the Terraform config alignment email.
OE 12: Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for each client-specific validation rule Anh needs to implement, assigned to team_engineering. Tickets should include: (a) Vectral Systems validation rules ergonomic $600 limit, furniture $400, internet $150, plus Camille Oduya $3,500 enhanced per-employee override; (b) Mosaic Robotics validation rules furniture/ergonomic $800 cap, coworking excluded category as array; (c) BrightLoop Analytics validation rules standard limits from existing submissions; (d) Terraform Digital onboarding configuration schema requirements for 1,200+ employee volume with tiered pricing. Each ticket should include actual limits, exclusions, and edge cases in the description.
OE 13: Add comments on Lena's existing config fix Linear tickets using linear_create_comment, referencing the new per-client validation rules being created and noting the Terraform onboarding schema requirements so she knows what's coming on the backend policy loader side.
OE 14: Post a summary message in #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_add_message describing the full policy configuration landscape across all stipend platform clients per-client limits, exclusions, per-employee overrides (Camille Oduya $3,500), and the new Terraform onboarding requirements so the team can review before Anh starts writing validation tests.
OE 15: Send an email from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com using send_email about the Terraform Digital config specifically covering the schema requirements for the new enterprise client (1,200+ employees, volume pricing tiers, self-serve platform), the per-employee override pattern needed for cases like Camille Oduya's enhanced stipend, and requesting alignment on the backend schema before Anh builds the editor screens for Terraform onboarding.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01) for clients currently on the stipend platform by looking for records with a non-null Monthly Stipend Fee using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records. Discover that 4 clients have active stipend subscriptions: Canopy Health ($2,000/month, AM: Catalina Dubois), Vectral Systems ($2,000/month, AM: Mina Hashimoto), GreenStack Energy ($2,000/month, AM: Emeka Diallo), and BrightLoop Analytics ($2,000/month, AM: Mina Hashimoto). Also discover Terraform Digital ($2,000/month, AM: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Onboarding).
OE 2: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) for Vectral Systems records using airtable_search_records to discover the actual policy limits that were applied. Key findings: ergonomic equipment limit is $600 (Danielle Osei's $475 chair approved; review notes reference "Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment"), furniture default was stale at $400 (Maria Santos $550 desk rejected despite $600 being the real limit), internet limit is $150 (Kevin Tran's $185 exceeded even the correct limit but was approved as goodwill), and coworking day passes were rejected per client agreement.
OE 3: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for Mosaic Robotics records using airtable_search_records to discover Mosaic's policy configuration. Key findings: furniture/ergonomic cap is $800 (Andre Washington's $1,200 desk auto-approved in error due to string comparison bug max_amount stored as string "800"), coworking is explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy (Andre Washington's $350 membership and Kenji Nakamura's $135 day passes both auto-approved due to excluded_categories stored as string instead of array).
OE 4: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for BrightLoop Analytics records using airtable_search_records to find BrightLoop's stipend submissions. Discover Jordan Ekwueme's approved $299.99 WiFi system and $45 monitor stand (flagged with PDF template match to the TechComfort Supply pattern), plus the Canopy Health and GreenStack stipend patterns for a complete picture of per-client limits.
OE 5: Search Airtable Relocations table (tblRelocations01) for Vectral Systems records using airtable_search_records to find Camille Oduya's relocation record (recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle) with the special requirement: "ENHANCED STIPEND: $3,500 home-office equipment stipend (vs. standard $2,000) negotiated as part of Camille's offer package MUST be configured in stipend platform from day one."
OE 6: Search CRM for Terraform Digital using crm_search_companies to find the company record, discovering it is also tracked internally as "Vantage Distributed" in legacy CRM records. Retrieve account details: 1,200+ employees, $85M annual revenue, Enterprise contract signed March 18, 2026, monthly stipend platform fee of $2,000, and 3 relocations requested (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi). Discover that volume pricing tiers are being formalized (standard rate for 1-10 moves/quarter, 8% discount at 11-20 moves).
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Terraform" or "onboarding" or "stipend platform" to find discussions about the Terraform Digital setup in #customer-engagement (C002) and #engineering (C003). Discover any threads about the stipend platform configuration needed for Terraform's 1,200+ employee self-serve onboarding and volume pricing tier requirements.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Terraform" or "stipend" or "Camille" or "enhanced stipend" to find correspondence about the Terraform Digital onboarding requirements (Rachel Nguyen's relocation requests) and any Vectral emails referencing Camille Oduya's negotiated $3,500 enhanced stipend arrangement.
OE 9: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "expense" or "config" or "policy" or "validation" in #engineering (C003) to find Lena Björkström's discussions about the config pipeline fixes (string-vs-number type mismatch for Vectral, excluded_categories string-vs-array for Mosaic) and Samira's launch readiness checklist discussions.
OE 10: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "expense" or "config" or "policy" to find Lena's existing config fix tickets (Vectral policy config fix and Mosaic config fix), Dmitri's audit ticket (47-expense audit), and ENG-220 (duplicate receipt detection). These are the tickets Anh needs to add comments to.
OE 11: Look up Lena Björkström's contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Lena" to retrieve her email address lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com for sending the Terraform config alignment email.
OE 12: Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for each client-specific validation rule Anh needs to implement, assigned to team_engineering. Tickets should include: (a) Vectral Systems validation rules ergonomic $600 limit, furniture $400, internet $150, plus Camille Oduya $3,500 enhanced per-employee override; (b) Mosaic Robotics validation rules furniture/ergonomic $800 cap, coworking excluded category as array; (c) BrightLoop Analytics validation rules standard limits from existing submissions; (d) Terraform Digital onboarding configuration schema requirements for 1,200+ employee volume with tiered pricing. Each ticket should include actual limits, exclusions, and edge cases in the description.
OE 13: Add comments on Lena's existing config fix Linear tickets using linear_create_comment, referencing the new per-client validation rules being created and noting the Terraform onboarding schema requirements so she knows what's coming on the backend policy loader side.
OE 14: Post a summary message in #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_add_message describing the full policy configuration landscape across all stipend platform clients per-client limits, exclusions, per-employee overrides (Camille Oduya $3,500), and the new Terraform onboarding requirements so the team can review before Anh starts writing validation tests.
OE 15: Send an email from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com using send_email about the Terraform Digital config specifically covering the schema requirements for the new enterprise client (1,200+ employees, volume pricing tiers, self-serve platform), the per-employee override pattern needed for cases like Camille Oduya's enhanced stipend, and requesting alignment on the backend schema before Anh builds the editor screens for Terraform onboarding.

28 The Mosaic Robotics Linear ticket (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) references that max_amount was stored as a string instead of a number (or similar numeric type-mismatch language).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Mosaic ticket description explicitly states 'max_amount must be a number, not a string. The original bug was "800" (string) instead of 800 (number), causing 1200 <= "800" to evaluate as true via lexicographic comparison'.
✓Run #2The Mosaic ticket description explicitly includes '`max_amount` stored as string `"800"` instead of number `800`' as a known bug, directly referencing the numeric type-mismatch.
✓Run #3The Mosaic ticket description explicitly states: 'max_amount stored as "800" (string) caused 1200 <= "800" to evaluate true via lexicographic comparison. Editor MUST enforce typeof max_amount === "number".' This directly references the numeric type mismatch.
✓Run #4The Mosaic ticket description explicitly states '`max_amount` was stored as `"800"` (string). `1200 <= "800"` evaluated `true` via JS lexicographic comparison' — clear string vs number type-mismatch documentation.
✓Run #5The Mosaic ticket description states '`max_amount` was stored as string "800" instead of number 800' and includes validation rule 'max_amount fields: strict numeric type check — typeof value === "number"'.
✓Run #6The Mosaic ticket description explicitly states '`max_amount` as string: The original config had `"800"` instead of `800`. JavaScript lexicographic comparison `1200 <= "800"` evaluates `true`... Editor MUST enforce `typeof max_amount === 'number'`'.
29 The Mosaic Robotics Linear ticket (visible in linear_create_issue parameters) references that excluded_categories was stored as a string instead of an array (or similar array type-mismatch language).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Mosaic ticket explicitly states 'excluded_categories must be a JSON array. The original bug was "coworking" (string) instead of ["coworking"] (array), causing the Array.isArray() check to return false'.
✓Run #2The Mosaic ticket description explicitly includes '`excluded_categories` stored as string `"coworking"` instead of array `["coworking"]`', directly referencing the array type-mismatch.
✓Run #3The Mosaic ticket description explicitly states: 'excluded_categories stored as "coworking" (string) caused Array.isArray() to return false, skipping the exclusion check entirely. Editor MUST enforce array type.' This directly references the array type mismatch.
✓Run #4The Mosaic ticket explicitly states '`excluded_categories` was stored as `"coworking"` (string). `Array.isArray("coworking")` returned `false` → exclusion check was skipped entirely' — clear array type-mismatch documentation.
✓Run #5The Mosaic ticket description states '`excluded_categories` was stored as string "coworking" instead of array ["coworking"]' with explicit array type-mismatch language.
✓Run #6The Mosaic ticket explicitly states '`excluded_categories` as string: Original config had `"coworking"` instead of `["coworking"]`. `Array.isArray("coworking")` returns `false`, so the exclusion check was skipped entirely. Editor MUST enforce array serialization.'

tool selection
